title: Alcohol Use Disorder Education/Prevention - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Alcohol Use Disorder Education/Prevention 44 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Hispanic/Latino Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ADDICTIONS RECOVERY CENTER OUTPATIENT TREATMENT AND MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient substance use disorder treatment, medication assisted treatment, problem gambling treatment, mental health counseling, and community education. 1025 E Main Street Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 779-1282 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HEALTH SERVICE BUILDING SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER TREATMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers individual and group counseling for people who want to change their relationship to substance use. Services may also include drug and alcohol assessments, drug screening, and case management. Peer support and recovery mentors are available, along with referrals to mental health and community resources. Also supports parent–child reunification, provides Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT), helps with admissions to detox and residential treatment, and creates relapse prevention plans to support long-term recovery. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday -Friday 8am-5pm (541) 766-6767 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF WESTERN TREASURE VALLEY HEALTH AND FITNESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Focuses on improving the overall health of Club Members by increasing their daily physical activity through recreational activities and team sports. Offers a prevention and education program that addresses issues such as drug and alcohol use and premature sexual activity. 573 SW 3rd Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Ages 6-18 Hours: Monday-Friday 2:45pm-6:30pm on regular school days (follows the Ontario School District calendar), Summer: Monday-Friday 7:45am-5:30pm, Orientation: Wednesday 5:30pm Email (541) 889-7979 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADIA HEALTH CASCADIA PLAZA HEALTH CENTER CASCADIA CENTRAL INTAKE AND REFERRAL LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an intake line for services such as case management, individual, family, group, geriatric and LGBTQ counseling, alcohol and drug treatment services, DUII and gambling addiction/treatment services, recovery housing services, sexual abuse counseling, domestic violence services, urgent mental health services, medication management, money management, outreach, crisis intervention, outpatient mental health services, and early intervention services. Provides about 1000 units in sites operating as licensed residential, permanent supported housing, and independent housing. 4212 SE Division Street Suites 100 and 200 Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 674-7777 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR HUMAN DEVELOPMENT ALCOHOL AND DRUG TREATMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services related to drug and alcohol addiction. Services include: individual, family, and group counseling, structured outpatient treatment, and DUII court mandated rehabilitation. 2301 Cove Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 962-8800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION ALCOHOL AND DRUG TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers alcohol and drug treatment services through a network of providers. Other services include domestic violence intervention services, moral reconation groups, anger management, dual diagnosis treatment, and urinalysis and other screening tools. Substance use disorder prevention education available for children, parents, and community members. Also provides gambling assessment and treatment. 702 Sunset Drive Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Assessment walk-in hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Office: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm, evening services available Email (541) 889-9167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY ALCOHOL AND DRUG PREVENTION TREATMENT OUTPATIENT ALCOHOL, DRUG, AND GAMBLING TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides prevention and treatment services for alcohol, drug, and gambling addiction.
